review:
I wanted to check out this grocery store and very glad I did. I was able to find some authentic Greek cheeses I had been missing from back home. In Chicago these cheeses are so easy to get and have so many different varieties than this brand. But this brand will do for the time being. Overpriced yes, but was able to purchase real feta made with sheep and goats milk, kasseri and mzyithra. This particular brand of Mt Vikos is PDO which I can appreciate knowing the origin and the authenticity. Mt Vikos brand for feta and kasseri I knew. But the mzyithra is not labeled to know who makes it says it comes from Mesa AZ. Also this piece of mizithra was made with pasteurized sheeps milk but should be raw plus I wanted the whole piece the entire round circle of it not just a chunk. The lady at cheese counter said the one who does the order was not at store so maybe next time I can find out.  

The store has a large variety of things. But I won't lie you can get alot of the same things alot lower priced elsewhere. The hard to find items not so much so it is definitely worth coming here to browse. They have an outdoor eating area for guests and misters on the lanai a very nice touch. 

Staff I came across was friendly and helpful. I was curious about the bakery and asked if items were made inhouse from scratch was told no..what a bummer. Store is clean and dim lit almost in a very odd spa/therapeutic way in which I liked and never noticed at any other grocer I been to except the ones saving on electricity..that have a 5 gallon bucket trying to catch rain leaks from the roof.  In which this was not the case here it was definitely an added touch to the shopping experience in a pleasant way.
